title: Topology Declarations, Policies, and Migration · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a versioned Go topology ownership manifest. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a versioned Go topology ownership manifest with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Make QueueDeclare and ExchangeDeclare calls equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and classify precondition errors.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 1559 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; reject stale connection and channel generations; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
